_meta.description: Reference: Avantis Investors' packaged allocation ETFs (the firm's published 'model' allocations), used as institutional-grade baseline weights to reconcile the CWS Planning IPS models in src/drift/firm_models.py against. This is a REFERENCE, not investment advice.
_meta.disclaimer: Avantis discloses top-level asset splits, benchmarks, and expense ratios on its fund pages; precise underlying fund-level weights live in the fact sheets / prospectuses and drift over time. Confirm every figure against the current Avantis fact sheet before any client use. Fields marked "_unverified" were not disclosed on the sourced pages and must not be presented as Avantis-published.

models.name: Avantis All Equity (AVGE)
models.benchmark: MSCI ACWI IMI (all-cap, 23 developed + 24 emerging markets)
models.factor_tilts: Actively tilts toward value, higher profitability, and smaller-cap within each region via Avantis's daily valuation screens.

models.name: Avantis Moderate Allocation (AVMA)
models.benchmark: 65% MSCI ACWI IMI / 35% Bloomberg Aggregate Gov/Credit 1-5 Year

equity_regional_breakdown._source: third-party review (optimizedportfolio.com/avma) — NOT an Avantis-published breakdown; confirm against the AVMA fact sheet

models.factor_tilts: Value / profitability / size tilts via the underlying Avantis funds.

other_packaged_options.name: Avantis Total Equity Markets
other_packaged_options.note: all-equity global; verify split + ER on the Avantis fund page

other_packaged_options.name: Avantis Global Equity Value
other_packaged_options.note: all-equity, value tilt; verify split + ER

_reconciliation_note: These Avantis packaged allocations are the institutional baseline for the CWS Planning IPS models in src/drift/firm_models.py. AVGE ~ the all-equity 'Aggressive Growth' sleeve; AVMA (65/35) ~ a 'Growth'/'Moderate' target. Reconcile firm_models weights and the individual-fund picks (AVUS/AVUV/AVDE/AVES/AVIG/BIL) against these before deployment; nothing here is a recommendation.
